note: Reprocessed with v3 domain-exhaustive pipeline. 34,763 pairs scored (vs v2's 629). Gap signals identified from FDEs where all scored mappings were strength 3 (weakest qualifying match). Of 10 gap signals, 1 is a genuine WIDAI gap (Staffing — existing KSA LS-T-011 scores below 0.35 threshold against its triggering FDE). 9 are out-of-scope or foundational O*NET elements. 6 of 7 KSAs created in v2 gap analysis are now top matches in their triggering FDEs, validating the gap resolution approach.

no_relationship_elements.assessment: All 18 No Relationship FDEs are genuinely outside WIDAI scope. No KSA creation warranted. 14 were identified as out-of-scope by domain routing (never scored). 4 additional scored but had no qualifying matches (all STS < 0.35).

elements.name: Philosophy and Theology
elements.reason: Humanities — outside data/AI scope (ethics covered in AG domain)

elements.name: Time Management
elements.reason: General workplace skill — foundational but not data/AI-specific

elements.name: Selective Attention
elements.reason: General cognitive ability — foundational but not data/AI-specific

elements.name: Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

gap_signals.title: Data/AI Organizational Staffing (PERSISTENT)
gap_signals.description: Recruiting, selecting, and hiring for data/AI teams. LS-T-011 was created in v2 gap analysis to address this, but scores below the 0.35 threshold against this FDE in v3 exhaustive scoring. LS-K-009 (talent management knowledge) remains the best match at strength 3. The LS-T-011 statement may need refinement to better align with O*NET staffing language.

v2_gap_resolution.v3_status: RESOLVED — LS-S-014 is now top match (STS 0.597, strength 5)
